speaker: Philip R. Lane
tone_summary: Lane's readable speeches in the 8-week window are notably light on direct monetary policy signalling. His March 23 AI speech is an academic analysis of AI as a general-purpose technology and its macroeconomic implications — no rate guidance. His February 12 Bulgaria speech is a welcome address for Bulgaria's euro entry, covering structural challenges and common policy issues without monetary policy direction. The February 9 monetary policy decision speech was only available as a PDF. Lane's characteristic style is analytical and balanced; his score reflects his structurally neutral/slightly dovish lean as Chief Economist, with no new hawkish signals evident in the readable content.
representative_quote: AI has the potential to also raise the productivity of the innovation process itself — not just shift the level of productive capacity but shift the rate at which productive capacity grows.
